commit.message:
fix: repair Appwrite document permissions so token join works

Old game documents were created without Permission.read(Role.any()),
causing getGameByToken() to return null for non-owners (Join button
stays disabled).

- Add userId field to Game type (populated by documentToGame)
- updateGame() now sets [read(any), update(user), delete(user)]
  permissions when userId is present, retroactively fixing old docs
  the next time the host saves any setting
- Ensures the Join button becomes enabled for a valid token

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: synergysquares
repository.description: SynergySquares is a customizable online Bingo game designed for meetings, town halls, and corporate events. Players can create and share Bingo boards filled with buzzwords and jargon, making discussions more engaging and interactive. Play solo or host a game with colleagues—when someone gets Bingo, everyone sees their winning pattern.

commit.message:
feat: wire game token join flow to Appwrite (task [4])

- Add Permission.read(Role.any()) to createGame() so any user can look up
  games by token via Appwrite query
- Add joinByToken() to GameContext: always queries Appwrite, appends the
  joining player to the local games list without writing to the host's doc
  (write permissions will be handled via Realtime in task [6])
- Replace handleJoinGame in game-settings-modal.tsx with joinByToken();
  uses authenticated user's name/id where available, guest fallback otherwise
- Update validateToken effect to call gameService.getGameByToken() directly
  so guests can validate tokens for games hosted by other users
- Mark tasks [3] and [4] done in TODOS.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: synergysquares
repository.description: SynergySquares is a customizable online Bingo game designed for meetings, town halls, and corporate events. Players can create and share Bingo boards filled with buzzwords and jargon, making discussions more engaging and interactive. Play solo or host a game with colleagues—when someone gets Bingo, everyone sees their winning pattern.

commit.message:
feat: migrate game-context.tsx from localStorage to Appwrite (task [2])

- Load games from Appwrite on user login (getUserGames); wipe on logout
- createGame() now async (returns Promise<string>) — awaits Appwrite
  document creation and uses the returned $id as the game ID
- updateGame() uses optimistic local update + background Appwrite sync
- removeGame() uses optimistic local remove + background Appwrite delete
- fetchGameByToken() delegates to gameService.getGameByToken (Appwrite)
- Add isLoading state (exposed on context) to signal Appwrite fetch
- Add isCreatingRef guard to prevent concurrent game creation calls
- Fix race condition: games only auto-created once user is resolved and
  isLoading is false (was previously creating anonymous games)
- Update game-dashboard.tsx: handleCreateGame is now async; auto-create
  effect respects isLoading and requires an authenticated user

Tests: add __tests__/game-utils.test.ts (25 tests) covering
generateBoardCells and getWinPatterns for 3x3, 5x5, 9x9 boards.
Total test suite: 57 tests, all passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Catalyst-Designer enzyme pathway optimizer

commit.message:
fix: FlowEditor バグ群を修正 (S-1~S-4, F-1~F-4, C-1~C-3, C-6, W-1~W-7)

Phase 1: 致命的バグ修正
- TimelineConfigV2 に queryPlan フィールド追加 (ラウンドトリップ保持)
- handleFlowSave 書き換え: QueryPlan直接保存、モデレーション/バックエンド抽出、ゴーストフィルタクリア
- FlowQueryEditorModal: 二重変換削除(F-1)、useEffect再初期化(F-2)、useRefカウンタ(F-4)

Phase 2: 変換ロジック修正
- flowToQueryPlan: 複数入力エッジ→暗黙マージ(C-1)、マージノード停止(C-2)、空マージガード(C-3)
- queryPlanToFlow: 動的Merge X座標(C-6)

Phase 3: WHERE ラウンドトリップ修正
- nodesToWhere: SQLエスケープ(W-7)、null処理、空配列ガード(W-5)、innerFilters対応(W-2)
- whereToNodes: NOT INパース(W-1)、ジェネリックEXISTSパーサ(W-4)、count-lte/eq対応(W-6)
- filterToSql: count-gte デフォルト値 0→1 修正(W-3)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add workload validate substitution expansion guide (#3521)

* docs: add workload validate substitution expansion guide

Documents how ksail workload validate handles Flux ${VAR} substitution
expansion — typed placeholders, defaults via :- and :=, env-var isolation,
schema inference, and practical guidance for avoiding false validation failures.

Fixes https://github.com/devantler-tech/ksail/issues/3519

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

* fix(docs): correct workload validate guide per PR review

- Fix factually incorrect NOTE: workload apply/push/reconcile do NOT
  expand ${VAR} from local env vars. They pass manifests through;
  Flux performs substitution in-cluster via postBuild.
- Fix frontmatter casing: use backticks for `workload validate` and
  capitalize KSail to match existing guide conventions.
- Clarify schema inference: typed placeholders require cached schemas.
  Core and CRD resources fall back to string "placeholder" when their
  schemas have not yet been cached.

---------

Signed-off-by: Nikolai Emil Damm <ned@devantler.tech>

repository.name: Nexus-bio-1.0
repository.description: A website that bridging the gap between biological literature and structural insight via AI-driven 3D visualization and metabolic modeling

commit.message:
feat(fbasim): multi-species community FBA with cross-feeding visualization

- Add S. cerevisiae metabolic model (10 reactions, 9 nodes, fermentation pathway)
- Implement calculateCommunityFlux() with composite S_com matrix coupling
  two strains through shared environmental pool exchange reactions
- Community biomass objective: weighted sum of strain growth rates with
  cross-feeding bonus from metabolite exchange (acetate, ethanol, succinate, lactate)
- Single/Community mode toggle with smooth sidebar transition
- Dual flux map visualization in community mode (E. coli + S. cerevisiae)
- Shared Metabolite Bus with animated directional flow lines
- Glassmorphism containers: Strain A (#DBCDF0), Strain B (#C9E4DE), Pool (#C6DEF1)
  with backdrop-filter blur(10px) and border-radius 24px
- Real-time cross-strain coupling: knocking out genes in Strain A updates
  Strain B growth via exchange flux recalculation
- JetBrains Mono for metrics, Inter for labels, right-aligned flux values
- Add CommunityFBAResult, StrainConfig, ExchangeReaction types

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Prevent browser zoom so Cmd+0 is never needed

- viewport: add maximum-scale=1.0 user-scalable=no to lock mobile zoom
- block wheel+ctrl/meta (trackpad pinch-to-zoom) and Safari gesture events
  so the page stays at 100% and never needs manual zoom reset

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix tok/s and ETA calculations on resume

Both metrics were wrong after resume:
- tok/s used total tokens_seen (including pre-resume) / elapsed_this_run
  → showed inflated numbers that decayed over time
- ETA used elapsed / step (total steps including pre-resume)
  → showed increasing ETA instead of decreasing

Fix: track start_step and start_tokens at run start, compute metrics
from deltas only. Phase 2 already did this correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix calibration trial to match real ACT training loop

The trial function was much simpler than the actual training, causing the
calibrator to overestimate safe batch sizes (calibrated 157, OOM'd to 45).

Changes:
- Trial now replicates full ACT halt-weighting (remaining, w, weighted_logits)
- Creates temp optimizer and runs a step so AdamW momentum/variance buffers
  are allocated during peak measurement (adds ~256MB baseline)
- Matches streaming_act_forward() structure exactly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test(web): parser mode sync and resize content preservation

Add 15 new tests to WebTerminal covering two previously untested areas:

Parser mode sync (9 tests):
- DECSET/DECRST 1 (application cursor keys) sync to InputHandler
- DECSET/DECRST 2004 (bracketed paste) sync to InputHandler
- DECSET/DECRST 1000 (VT200 mouse) sync mouse protocol to InputHandler
- DECSET 1004 (focus events) sync to InputHandler
- DECSET/DECRST 1049 (alternate buffer) re-attaches renderer to new grid

Resize content preservation (6 tests):
- Content preserved when growing rows
- Content preserved when growing columns
- Content preserved when shrinking rows (cursor in range)
- Content shift when cursor exceeds new row count (srcStartRow logic)
- Cursor column clamped when shrinking columns
- Resize is idempotent when dimensions unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Level 2 blocked path: vwall(19) now stops at row 14, not 16

The left wall of connector 2 extended into the middle trail (rows 15-16)
at col 19, completely sealing the trail and blocking all forward progress.
Fixed by ending vwall(19) at row 14 so the middle corridor is fully open.

Also tuned momentum physics: FRICTION 0.80→0.84, MAX_VEL 2.6→2.4,
control penalty 55%→35% so turns in narrow connectors are navigable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix path blocking: collapsed cells are damage zones, not walls

Collapsed cells no longer block movement (removed from isWallOrClosedGate).
The path can never be blocked regardless of how many cells collapse.

Instead, stepping on a collapsed cell:
- Triggers loseLife() + invincibility frames
- Reverses momentum (bounces Glory away from pit)
- Visual pit still shows (dark + red glow) as a clear 'avoid this' signal

This preserves all the tension and visual drama of crumbling ground
while making it impossible for Glory to get stuck.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Level 2 emotional theme: panic, dread, and speed tension

'I might fall any second…'
- playCrack() audio: deep sawtooth groan + noise burst when a cell
  starts cracking (structural sound of ground giving way)
- Screen shake 0.004 on crack start, 0.009 on full collapse
- Abyss vignette (dark at bottom) when Glory is within 2 cells of a
  cracking/collapsed tile — subconscious dread of falling

'They're getting closer…'
- Left-edge red danger gradient scales with nearest hunter distance
  (8 cells → 0 alpha, 0 cells → full bleed). 5 bands fade rightward.
- Proximity tremor: random micro-shake (0.003) when hunter ≤ 3 cells

'I can't stop…'
- Speed lines: white streaks trail behind Glory's movement direction
  when velocity > 1.4 px/frame. More lines and brighter at max speed.
- Wind gust now shakes camera (0.004) on first hit each gust

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Level 2: unstable path, constant chase, zone-based wind

UNSTABLE PATH
- 14 trail cells cycle: stable → cracking (1.8s warning) → collapsed
  (3.5s pit blocks movement) → recovering (1.2s) → stable (7-13s wait)
- Staggered timers so cells crack at different times (never all at once)
- Cracking: flickering amber crack lines warn player to move
- Collapsed: dark pit with red-orange glow, counts as a wall — Glory
  can't step through and falls if standing on cell when it collapses
- Recovering: grey dust fades back to normal ground
- collapsedCells Set added to isWallOrClosedGate() check

CONSTANT CHASE
- Hunters that retreat back to col ≤ 1 are immediately re-energised:
  retreating cleared, teleported to left edge on Glory's row → chase
  resumes with zero downtime — no safe zone to rest in

WIND PUSH ZONES
- Connector 1 (cols 12-16, x 240-320) and Connector 2 (cols 19-23,
  x 380-460) are designated strong-wind zones: drift force = 1.5×
  vs 0.7× on the sheltered trail sections
- Gust frequency increased slightly (every 4-7.5s instead of 5-9s)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Level 2 balance mechanic: momentum + speed-based sliding

- Level 2 uses velocity-based movement instead of direct position updates
  - gloryVx/gloryVy accumulate from joystick input (ACCEL=0.28)
  - FRICTION=0.80 per frame → Glory slides when input stops or reverses
  - MAX_VEL=2.6 px/frame caps momentum
- Control factor scales from 1.0 (stopped) → 0.45 (full speed):
  steering response drops 55% at max velocity, making high-speed turns
  sloppy and hard to correct — exactly the 'harder to control' feeling
- Wall collision: hitting a wall kills 80% of velocity in that axis
- All other levels retain direct, snappy movement unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Level 2 gameplay theme: balance, forward pressure, edge danger

- EDGE DANGER: 12 precarious ledge cells along trail margins, visually
  marked with pulsing amber glow + crack lines. Stepping on one shows
  'CRUMBLING EDGE!' warning; standing for 1.4s causes Glory to fall
  (lose a life). Cells glow in drawLevel2Background().
- FORWARD PRESSURE: If Glory hasn't advanced for 2.5s, snakes get 40%
- BALANCE: Wind push already active (from prior commit).
- All three together enforce: keep moving, stay on safe path, don't
  get caught — the three core rules of the gameplay theme.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Level 2: snakes start behind Glory, add banana/berry variety

- Hunters now spawn at col 0 (off left edge, behind Glory's start col 1)
  so they chase from behind rather than blocking the path ahead
- Level 2 collectibles now include apples, bananas, and berries instead
  of all-apple defaults (FruitKind added to each tuple)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Level 2 immersion: heartbeat, rock falls, wind push, hiss/footsteps on trail

- Heartbeat sound (lub-DUB) + red pulsing vignette when lives = 1 (all levels)
- Falling rocks: spawn in cliff zones, animate into abyss with scrape audio

Trail layout (S-curve, 2 cells wide throughout):
- Upper-left corridor (rows 5-6, cols 1-15) → entrance
- Connector 1 (cols 12-15) goes DOWN to rows 7-14
- Middle corridor (rows 15-16, cols 12-21) → choke point
- Connector 2 (cols 20-22) goes UP back to rows 6-16
- Upper-right corridor (rows 5-6, cols 22-30) → exit at col 30

Snake enemies (4 total):
- 🔴 Hunter (tickMs=380): chases from behind at col 5, row 5
- 🟢 Hunter (tickMs=430): staggered pursuer at col 4, row 6
- 🟡 Patrol (tickMs=460): sweeps the middle choke, cols 13-20
- 🟠 Sentry (tickMs=340): guards near exit at col 26, charges in radius 5

Other details:
- glorySpeed: 2.0, speedRamp: true, scoreMultiplier: 1.2
- 10 collectibles along the route, 5 bushes for brief cover
- Banner: '🌄 NARROW TRAIL — They followed you — RUN! 🏃‍♀️'

commit.message:
feat: Fruit House Trap — maze, stealth, puzzle, tools, escape rush & tension

- Replace Level 1 mountain path with indoor Fruit House Trap layout
  (4-room cross-plan, narrow hallways, dead-end alcoves, 6 snakes)
- Fruit power-ups: apple=health, banana=speed boost, berry=invisibility
- Three snake types: 🟢 sleeper, 🟡 patrol, 🔴 sentry
- Stealth system: slow joystick / Shift key = sneak mode (👣 HUD indicator)
- Mini fruit puzzle locks the exit (solve correctly or snakes go BERSERK 5s)
- Hidden golden key in SE room bypasses the puzzle entirely
- Fruit gate: must collect 4+ fruits before puzzle triggers
- Escape rush: on exit unlock all snakes turn hunter and charge (🏃
- Survival tools via Susie: 🪵 Wood Stick (stun+retreat), �� Smoke Bomb
  (5s full invisibility), 🪤 Trap (already existed, now in house rotation)
- Tension: indoor dim-lighting vignette (darker during escape rush),
  procedural Web Audio hiss when snake < 5 cells, soft footstep clicks
  while sneaking, jump-scare camera shake + stab sound when sleeper wakes,
  ascending chime on exit unlock, ⚡ BERSERK / 🏃‍♀️ RUN HUD indicators

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Disable torch.compile for Phase 5 — inductor can't partition ACT graph

The ACT loop accumulates 4 forward passes into weighted_logits, creating
a computation graph that PyTorch 2.10's inductor partitioner cannot handle
(AssertionError: Node add_N was invalid, but is output). This is unrelated
to gradient checkpointing — the multi-pass accumulation pattern itself is
incompatible. Phases 1/3/4 compile fine with their single-pass graphs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Auto-resume, torch.compile for all phases, Gen 1 notebook cells

Resume:
- All phases now auto-detect latest.pt and resume without --resume flag
- Shows 'Resumed from step N (XM tokens)' on auto-resume
- Phase 5 exits early with message if training already complete
- Colab sessions no longer lose progress on re-run

torch.compile:
- Added to Phase 3 and Phase 4 (were missing, only Phase 1 had it)
- Standardized all phases to mode='default' (reduce-overhead caused
  CUDA graph conflicts)
- Phase 2 skipped (only trains small address head MLPs, no benefit)
- Phase 4 VRAM target lowered to 70% (has ACT loop like Phase 5)

Notebook:
- Replaced generic Phase 5 cells with proper Gen 1 workflow:
  Step 1: Generate synthetic QA data
  Step 2: Pre-seed memory with knowledge base
  Step 3: Phase 5a (frozen memory, learn to read)
  Step 4: Phase 5b (unfrozen, learn to read+write)
- Kept TinyStories alternative as commented cell
- Each step auto-resumes, just re-run the cell

fix: resolve all TypeScript build errors + cross-platform dev script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Remove ACT checkpointing, lower VRAM target to 70%, re-enable torch.compile

ACT-level gradient checkpointing doubled forward compute (recompute during
backward) for only marginal VRAM savings — the calibrator should instead find
a smaller batch that fits without checkpointing.

Changes:
- Remove torch.utils.checkpoint from streaming_act_forward (direct forward)
- Remove checkpoint wrapper from hardware.py trial function
- Lower target_vram_frac from 0.80 to 0.70 (safe margin for ACT peak)
- Re-enable torch.compile (no longer conflicts without checkpointing)
- Add periodic empty_cache every 100 steps to defragment VRAM

Expected: calibrator finds micro_batch≈80-90 on A100-80GB, ~38K+ tok/s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: LatentController

commit.message:
Use torch.compile mode='default' instead of 'reduce-overhead'

reduce-overhead uses CUDA graphs which conflict with gradient
checkpointing and the mixed no_grad/grad ACT training loop. Default
mode still does kernel fusion but without CUDA graph constraints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Enable torch.compile in Phase 5 training

Was only used in Phase 1. Adds ~30s warmup on first step but speeds up
all subsequent steps via kernel fusion and reduced Python overhead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Checkpoint ACT steps to cut peak VRAM from ~85GB to ~30GB

The streaming ACT loop was holding computation graphs from ALL 4 forward
passes simultaneously for backward, causing 55GB of activation memory.
Now each ACT step is wrapped in torch.utils.checkpoint.checkpoint() —
only one step's activations are in memory at a time, recomputed during
backward. This should allow 3-4× larger batch sizes on the same GPU.

Also: trial function in build_trial_fn now uses matching checkpointing
so calibration accurately predicts training VRAM. Removed misleading
torch.compile warmup message (compile was never applied in Phase 5).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix bugs found during codebase audit (#407)

* Fix bugs found during codebase audit

Fix OrderedMap.size not updating on set/delete/clear, and keys() bypassing
insertion order. Fix ImmutableSet.clear() mutating internal state instead of
returning a new instance. Fix lexer retokenizeLines passing stale line number
to tokenize and remove dead offsetLineNumber variable. Fix localization mapping
KeywordHashShared to wrong template (keywordShared instead of keywordHashShared),
KeywordNot to wrong template (notEqual instead of keywordNot), and locale code
ca_EZ typo (should be ca_ES). Fix isTRelationalExpression return type narrowing
to TEqualityExpression instead of TRelationalExpression. Fix traverseRecursion
missing trace.exit() on early exit and exception paths via try/finally. Fix
recalculateIds comment claiming BFS when implementation is DFS. Fix typos in
taskUtils (acutalTaskStage), contextUtils (ore/replcementId), disambiguationUtils
(corrosponding), and lexer comments (propegation/accomodate/existing existing).
Add TypeKinds comment clarifying intentional exclusion of internal-only types.
Add comprehensive tests for OrderedMap, ImmutableSet, and lexer retokenization.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* added package bump

---------

Co-authored-by: Jordan Bolton <jobolton@microsoft.com>

Fix training loop: proper for/else, better logging, OOM-safe optimizer step

  occurred (previously ran scaler.unscale_ on broken gradient state after
  OOM break)

- Print torch.compile warmup warning (explains 1-3 min silence)

- Eval/save blocks properly nested inside else (skip after OOM)
- Also rebuild val_loader on OOM to keep eval batch in sync

commit.message:
Fix eval OOM: reduce eval batch to 1/4 training batch, clear VRAM cache

Eval does 1 forward pass for memory addresses + 4 ACT forward passes = 5×
the memory of a single training step. Using the training micro_batch for
eval guaranteed OOM on A100 40GB. Now uses micro_batch//4 for eval, adds
torch.cuda.empty_cache() before eval, and explicitly frees intermediate
tensors in the eval loop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(web): Kitty keyboard protocol disambiguate mode (flag 1)

When kittyFlags & 1 (disambiguate escape codes) is set via setKittyFlags():
- Escape → CSI 27 u (removes ambiguity with ESC-prefix sequences)
- Modifier+letter → CSI codepoint ; mod+1 u  (ctrl/alt combos)
- Shift+Tab → CSI 9 ; 2 u
- Modified cursor/special keys → CSI 1 ; mod+1 <letter>
- Modified tilde-style keys → CSI n ; mod+1 ~
- Unmodified keys retain legacy sequences

Added 29 tests in kitty-disambiguate.test.ts covering all encoding
paths and the setKittyFlags(0) legacy-restore path.

Full test suite: 1299 passed (was 1174).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Split KV-cache: separate memory and text caches for ACT speedup

Memory K,V and text K,V are now cached independently. ACT re-reads
only recompute memory K,V (11 positions via forward_memory) + reprocess
the last text token (1 position), instead of full forward (11+T positions).

Key changes:
- model.py: add forward_memory() — processes memory through all layers
  independently (exact, since memory rows can't attend to text)
- agent.py: maintain mem_kv_cache and text_kv_cache separately, with
  three modes: prefill, incremental, and ACT re-read

Performance: ACT=2 now matches ACT=1 speed (~115 tok/s on CPU).
Previously ACT=2 was 4x slower due to full re-forward on memory change.
Training path unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: LatentController
repository.description: Experimental small language model

commit.message:
Add KV-cache for incremental decoding (10x speedup)

Attention, TransformerBlock, and forward() now support optional kv_cache
parameter. During generation, previously-computed K,V are cached and
reused — only the new token runs through the model.

Results on M4 MPS:
- Before: 3-7 tok/s (reprocessed entire context per token)
- ACT=2: ~8 tok/s (cache invalidated on ACT re-read)
- ACT=1: ~32 tok/s (pure incremental decode)

Training is unaffected — kv_cache defaults to None, return types
unchanged when cache is not used.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CLI to find latest.pt when best.pt is missing (Phase 4)

Checkpoint search order per phase: best.inference.pt → best.pt →
latest.inference.pt → latest.pt. Also finds Phase 2 addr_heads.pt.
Shows which file was loaded in the startup log.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix generation: temperature sampling, remove BOS insertion, reduce ACT

Three fixes for the disappointing CLI output:

1. Remove BOS insertion in generate() that was resetting context,
   causing model to always start 'Once upon a time' regardless of input
2. Add temperature (default 0.8) and top-k (default 50) sampling to
   prevent 'a a a a a' degenerate greedy repetition
3. Reduce default ACT steps from 4 to 2 for faster generation

Note: The model still only knows TinyStories - it will generate
children's stories, not chat. This is expected at Phase 3/4.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: LatentController

commit.message:
Add --quantize flag to CLI (q8/q4 dynamic quantization)

Supports: --quantize q8 (int8, ~34MB) and --quantize q4 (int4, ~17MB).
Uses PyTorch native dynamic quantization on all nn.Linear layers.
Quantized models run on CPU (PyTorch limitation for dynamic quant).

Usage: python cli.py --quantize q8
       python cli.py --quantize q4 -p 'Once upon a time'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: LatentController

commit.message:
Save inference-only checkpoints (~136MB vs ~408MB) and prefer them in CLI

save_checkpoint() now also writes a .inference.pt file containing only
model weights (no optimizer/scaler state). AdamW stores 2 extra copies
of all parameters (momentum + variance), so full checkpoints are ~3x
the model size.

CLI --download now prefers .inference.pt files. load_model() checks for
inference checkpoints first, falls back to full checkpoints.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: LatentController

commit.message:
Fix --download to only fetch checkpoints and tokenizer, not training data

Previously downloaded data_cache/** which includes ~7GB of tokenized
training data. Now only downloads checkpoints/**/best.pt and
data_cache/tokenizer.json (~100MB total).
